summary: Review of changes addressing berendt's external review #2 feedback. All 6 findings (4 warnings, 2 info) were addressed: W-001 fail guard added to role.yaml, W-002 image.tag=latest replaced with v0.1.0, W-003 broken CRD install command replaced with direct kubectl apply, W-004 Chainsaw E2E test added, I-001 RBAC rules extracted into shared named template with reciprocal cross-reference, I-002 startup log line added for namespace-scoped mode.

tests_checklist.item: All helm unittest tests pass (70 passed, 0 failed)
Go unit tests pass (9 tests in bootstrap package)
Fail guard test covers rbac.namespaceScoped=true + webhook.enabled=true rejection
All role_test.yaml tests set webhook.enabled=false alongside rbac.namespaceScoped=true
E2E test follows existing Chainsaw pattern (SPDX header, step comments, JMESPath conditions, catch blocks)
Edge cases covered (fail guard, both RBAC modes verified via helm template)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (Chainsaw test structure, Helm template conventions)
RBAC rules deduplicated via shared named template keystone-operator.rbacRules
No dead code or unnecessary complexity
Clear naming and comments with feature ID references

security_checklist.item: Fail guard prevents invalid rbac.namespaceScoped=true + webhook.enabled=true combination
No hardcoded secrets in E2E fixtures or documentation
Documentation correctly recommends disabling webhooks in namespace-scoped mode

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — minimal changes per finding
Shared RBAC rules template eliminates duplication between clusterrole.yaml and role.yaml
CRD install command uses direct kubectl apply (works end-to-end)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Helm fail guard rejects incompatible configuration at template render time
Namespace-scoped mode logged at startup for debugging

defensive_checklist.item: E2E test validates both RBAC resource presence and ClusterRole absence

title: Add namespace-scoped RBAC and namespace flag
verdict_determination: APPROVED — All blocking checks pass: V1 (go test passes), V2 (go vet clean), V3 (golangci-lint clean), C1-C5 (all review feedback addressed correctly), S1-S6 (no security issues), T1-T5 (Go types correct), FC1-FC4 (all 6 findings implemented), PC1-PC3 (patterns followed). All major checks pass: TE1-TE10 (tests cover happy path, edge cases, E2E added), Q1-Q7 (clean code), DA1-DA3 (docs match implementation). Helm unittest 70/70 pass. Helm lint passes both configurations. Helm template produces correct resources for both modes. Go unit tests 9/9 pass. Fail guard correctly rejects incompatible configuration.
